---
title: "Session 07 teaching notes: Python 4"
session_id: session-07
event_id: null
---

## Session 7 â€” Pivoting, prediction, and data types

Python-Teil: Vorsichtig vorgehen (mÃ¶glichst viele Studis mitnehmen)

Pivot: see `2_Pivot.py` 

See `4_Python.py` (contains materials for the following sessions: TODDO: split.)

pp. 69: Prediction: `3_Predictions.py`

p.84/85: 4,5,6: homework

p.82 solution:

| Value    | Type        | To Type | Using function      | New value     |
| -------- | ----------- | ------- | ------------------- | ------------- |
| `"3"`    | **`str`**   | `int`   | **`int("3")`**      | **`3`**       |
| `"3.14"` | **`str`**   | `float` | **`float("3.14")`** | **`3.14`**    |
| `3`      | **`int`**   | `str`   | **`str(3)`**        | **`"3"`**     |
| `3.14`   | **`float`** | `str`   | **`str(3.14)`**     | **`"3.14"`**  |
| `False`  | **`bool`**  | `str`   | **`str(False)`**    | **`"False"`** |
| `"True"` | **`str`**   | `bool`  | **`bool("True")`**  | **`True`**    |
| `17.75`  | **`float`** | `int`   | **`int(17.75)`**    | **`17`**      |
| `"3"`    | **`str`**   | `float` | **`float("3")`**    | **`3.0`**     |

::: {.teaching-break}
â˜• Break â€” 10 minutes
:::

## Exercises

| Page(s) | Exercise                   | Summary                                                                                                                            | Files                                                                                                   | In-class / take-home |
| ------: | -------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
|      67 | Pivot tables               | Construct insurance pivot tables with multiple grouping dimensions, totals, filters, and a bar chart.                              | `insurance.xlsx`<br>`insurance2.py`                                                                     |                      |
|   74â€“75 | Chipotle analysis          | Inspect the dataset; calculate orders, quantities, prices, and revenue; identify popular items; and create charts.                 | `Chipotle.csv`<br>`Chipotle.py`                                                                         |                      |
|   76â€“77 | Florida housing analysis   | Calculate and analyze profits, compare agencies and house characteristics, and build three predictive models.                      | `Homes_Florida.xlsx`<br>`Homes_Florida.py`                                                              |                      |
|   84â€“85 | Input and formula programs | Create interactive programs for circle area, currencies, geographic distance, travel time, fuel consumption, and annuity payments. | `CircleArea2.py`<br>`Currency.py`<br>`Distance.py`<br>`Traveltime.py`<br>`Fuel.py`<br>`AnnuityLoan1.py` |                      |


## Summary and announcements (next session)

TODO


<!--
    ## Session overview

    - **Focus:** Python
    - **Session title:** Python 1
    - **Coverage target:** Until **Accessing data**

    ## Before class

    - [ ] Review the session slides/materials.
    - [ ] Prepare files, datasets, and examples.
    - [ ] Note any announcements or reminders.

    ## Timing record

    Use this table while preparing or after teaching to record the actual timing.

    | Planned time | Actual time | Topic/activity | Notes for next time |
    |---|---|---|---|
    | 0--10 min |  | Setup / recap |  |
    | 10--30 min |  | Main input |  |
    | 30--60 min |  | Guided practice |  |
    | 60--80 min |  | Independent practice / questions |  |
    | 80--90 min |  | Wrap-up |  |

    ## Notes during class

    - TBD

    ## Follow-up

    - [ ] Update examples or instructions.
    - [ ] Record common questions.
    - [ ] Add reminders for the next session.
-->
